expectations.text: Recommends `winml analyze` against the exported ONNX as the first diagnostic step.
expectations.evidence: No mention of winml analyze. Goes straight to verbose logging + onnxsim + opset checks.

expectations.text: Explains analyze will name the offending op pattern per EP.
expectations.evidence: Same â€” no analyze tool in the response.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend hand-editing the ONNX graph.
expectations.evidence: Doesn't suggest direct ONNX graph editing. (Does suggest patching PyTorch model code with `torch.cat`/slice replacements for `torch.roll` â€” invasive source-model surgery, but not ONNX-graph editing.)

expectations.text: Suggests trying different optim/quant config OR escalating to 'not a fit'.
expectations.evidence: Offers multiple paths: onnxsim, pin opset, simplification, CPU fallback for offending subgraph (hybrid execution).

expectations.text: Encourages reading actual error message before guessing.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 is 'Pin down the exact failing op/pattern' via verbose logs, and the response ends with 'Can you paste the exact error line from `winml optimize`?'.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable — every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All 1 winml commands verified runnable against `--help`.
